name: FFT
doc: fast fourier transform, converts input data from the time to the frequency domain and stores the result in a buffer (audio waveform -> graph equalizer bands) Output is -1 except when an FFT frame is ready, when the output is the buffer index. This creates a special kind of slower pseudo-rate (built on top of control rate) which all the pv-ugens understand.

argDocs.buffer: The buffer where a frame will be held. Its size must be a power of two. local-buf is useful here, because processes should not share data between synths. (Note: most PV UGens operate on this data in place. Use buffer-2n if you wish to create an external buffer.
argDocs.in: the signal to be analyzed. The signal's rate determines the rate at which the input is read.
argDocs.hop: the amount of offset from one FFT analysis frame to the next, measured in multiples of the analysis frame size. This can range between zero and one, and the default is 0.5 (meaning each frame has a 50% overlap with the preceding/following frames).
argDocs.wintype: defines how the data is windowed: RECT is for rectangular windowing, simple but typically not recommended; SINE (the default) is for Sine windowing, typically recommended for phase-vocoder work; HANN is for Hann windowing, typically recommended for analysis work.
argDocs.active: is a simple control allowing FFT analysis to be active (>0) or inactive (<=0). This is mainly useful for signal analysis processes which are only intended to analyse at specific times rather than continuously
argDocs.winsize: the windowed audio frames are usually the same size as the buffer. If you wish the FFT to be zero-padded then you can specify a window size smaller than the actual buffer size (e.g. window size 1024 with buffer size 2048). Both values must still be a power of two. Leave this at its default of zero for no zero-padding.

name: FFTTrigger
doc: Outputs the necessary signal for FFT chains, without doing an FFT on a signal

argDocs.buffer: a buffer to condition for FFT use
argDocs.hop: the hop size for timing triggers
argDocs.polar: a flag. If 0.0, the buffer will be prepared for complex data, if > 0.0, polar data is set up.

name: IFFT
doc: inverse fast fourier transform, converts buffer data from frequency domain to time domain The IFFT UGen converts the FFT data in-place (in the original FFT buffer) and overlap-adds the result to produce a continuous signal at its output.

argDocs.chain: The FFT chain signal coming originally from an FFT UGen, perhaps via other PV UGens.
argDocs.wintype: defines how the data is windowed: RECT is for rectangular windowing, simple but typically not recommended; SINE (the default) is for Sine windowing, typically recommended for phase-vocoder work; HANN is for Hann windowing, typically recommended for analysis work.
argDocs.winsize: can be used to account for zero-padding, in the same way as the FFT UGen.

name: PV_Add
doc: complex addition: RealA + RealB, ImagA + ImagB

name: PV_BinScramble
doc: randomizes the order of the bins. The trigger will select a new random ordering.

argDocs.wipe: scrambles more bins as wipe moves from zero to one.
argDocs.width: a value from zero to one, indicating the maximum randomized distance of a bin from its original location in the spectrum.
argDocs.trig: a trigger selects a new random ordering.

name: PV_BinShift
doc: shift and scale the positions of the bins. Can be used as a very crude frequency shifter/scaler.

argDocs.shift: add an offset to bin position.

name: PV_BinWipe
doc: copies low bins from one input and the high bins of the other

argDocs.wipe: can range between -1 and +1; if wipe == 0 then the output is the same as inA; if wipe > 0 then it begins replacing with bins from inB from the bottom up;if wipe < 0 then it begins replacing with bins from inB from the top down.

name: PV_BrickWall
doc: clears bins above or below a cutoff point

argDocs.wipe: can range between -1 and +1. if wipe == 0 then there is no effect; if wipe > 0 then it acts like a high pass filter, clearing bins from the bottom up; if wipe < 0 then it acts like a low pass filter, clearing bins from the top down.

name: PV_Conj
doc: converts the FFT frames to their complex conjugate (i.e. reverses the sign of their imaginary part). This is not usually a useful audio effect in itself, but may be a component of other analysis or transformation processes...

name: PV_Copy
doc: copies the spectral frame in bufferA to bufferB at that point in the chain of PV UGens. This allows for parallel processing of spectral data without the need for multiple FFT UGens, and to copy out data at that point in the chain for other purposes. bufferA and bufferB must be the same size.

name: PV_CopyPhase
doc: combines magnitudes of first input and phases of the second input

name: PV_Diffuser
doc: adds a different constant random phase shift to each bin. The trigger will select a new set of random phases.

argDocs.trig: a trigger selects a new set of random values.

name: PV_Div
doc: complex division

name: PV_LocalMax
doc: passes only bins whose magnitude is above a threshold and above their nearest neighbors

name: PV_MagAbove
doc: passes only bins whose magnitude is above a threshold

name: PV_MagBelow
doc: passes only bins whose magnitude is below a threshold

name: PV_MagClip
doc: clips bin magnitudes to a maximum threshold

name: PV_MagDiv
doc: divides magnitudes of two inputs and keeps the phases of the first input

argDocs.zeroed: number to use when bins are zeroed out, i.e. causing division by zero

name: PV_MagFreeze
doc: freezes magnitudes at current levels when freeze > 0

argDocs.freeze: if freeze > 0 then magnitudes are frozen at current levels.

name: PV_MagMul
doc: multiplies magnitudes of two inputs and keeps the phases of the first input

name: PV_MagNoise
doc: magnitudes are multiplied with noise

name: PV_MagShift
doc: shift and stretch the positions of only the magnitude of the bins. Can be used as a very crude frequency shifter/scaler.

argDocs.shift: add an offset to bin position.

name: PV_MagSmear
doc: average a bin's magnitude with its neighbors

argDocs.bins: number of bins to average on each side of bin. As this number rises, so will CPU usage.

name: PV_MagSquared
doc: squares the magnitudes and renormalizes to previous peak. This makes weak bins weaker.

name: PV_Max
doc: output copies bins with the maximum magnitude of the two inputs

name: PV_Min
doc: output copies bins with the minimum magnitude of the two inputs

name: PV_Mul
doc: complex multiplication: (RealA * RealB) - (ImagA * ImagB) (ImagA * RealB) + (RealA * ImagB)

name: PV_PhaseShift
doc: shift phase of all bins

name: PV_PhaseShift270
doc: shift phase of all bins by 270 degrees

name: PV_PhaseShift90
doc: shift phase of all bins by 90 degrees

name: PV_RandComb
doc: randomly clear bins

argDocs.wipe: clears bins from input in a random order as wipe goes from 0 to 1.
argDocs.trig: a trigger selects a new random ordering.

name: PV_RandWipe
doc: cross fades between two sounds by copying bins in a random order

argDocs.wipe: copies bins from bufferB in a random order as wipe goes from 0 to 1.
argDocs.trig: a trigger selects a new random ordering.

name: PV_RectComb
doc: makes a series of gaps in a spectrum

argDocs.numTeeth: number of teeth in the comb.

name: PV_RectComb2
doc: alternates blocks of bins between the two inputs

argDocs.numTeeth: number of teeth in the comb.
